_meta.name: wpr-finance-tools local constants
_meta.update_process: market.* from wpr-property-transactions ledger; property_tax.* verified against Marathon County Treasurer mill-rate publication each December; insurance/loan defaults reviewed manually
_meta.contract_rule: Widgets import this file at build time. Every consuming widget MUST validate required keys at module load and throw on any missing key. No runtime fetch, no fallback values.

median_sale_price.note: Median of 207 single-family sales, including 22 vacant-lot sales (median $62,000) — built homes alone ran $271,000.
median_sale_price.source: wpr-property-transactions ledger, data/transactions.json generated 2026-06-18 (DOR RETR; editorial policy applied in-pipeline: genuine sales only, >= $1,000; filtered to county = Marathon, property_use = Single family)

property_tax.note: Effective full-value rates: (total property tax - school levy credit) / DOR equalized value. Source: WI DOR 'Town, Village and City Taxes 2025' (https://www.revenue.wi.gov/SLFReportscotvc/tvc25.PDF), column 8, taxes levied 2025 / collected 2026. Excludes lottery and first-dollar credits, so slightly high for owner-occupants. All 61 Marathon County tax districts listed; municipalities straddling a county line are rated on their Marathon County portion and labeled as such. First entry is the calculator's default selection. Every rate machine-verified against the report's raw tax/credit/value columns; sums reconcile to the printed county totals to the dollar.

insurance.note: WI homeowners premium as share of home value. Derived from ~$1,390/yr on $300K dwelling (MoneyGeek/ValuePenguin 2026 WI averages). Statewide sources range $1,386–$2,465 depending on coverage assumptions; this uses the $300K-dwelling consensus cluster.

pmi.note: Applied when down payment < 20%. Industry-typical midpoint; not lender-specific.

closing_costs.note: Buyer-side estimate (lender, title, escrow, recording). WI real estate transfer fee ($3/$1,000) is seller-paid and excluded.

loan_defaults.note: UI starting values only — user-adjustable, so weekly rate movement doesn't require a deploy.
